objective: Convert the completed Remote MCP Stage D live proof into a recurring health and audit monitor with scheduled fixture coverage, configured live execution, payload-safe evidence scanning, runbook instructions, and governance closeout artifacts.
scope_boundary: Modify Remote MCP monitor scripts/tests, scheduled workflow, launchd template, Remote MCP runbook, governance registries, progress/backlog mirrors, issue #372 planning/validation/closeout artifacts, and generated graph/wiki artifacts.
Reuse the existing Stage D proof runner and audit verifier rather than changing Remote MCP invariants or duplicating protocol logic.
Do not commit Cloudflare credentials, bridge tokens, JWTs, HMAC keys, raw PII, or production audit payloads.
out_of_scope: Installing launchd on the operator machine as a side effect of this PR.
Adding permanent Cloudflare policies or secrets.
research_summary: Issue #109 closed after Stage A governance, downstream Stage B/C bridge controls, and final Stage D live Cloudflare proof. The follow-up operational gap is recurring health verification. Existing Stage D and audit verifier code already covers the required authenticated, negative, audit, tamper, and stdio checks, so issue #372 should compose those tools and add schedule/runbook surfaces plus payload-safe evidence scanning.

sprints.name: Recurring monitor implementation
sprints.goal: Add a recurring Remote MCP health monitor that reuses Stage D checks and proves fixture/live behavior safely.
tasks: Add live health monitor wrapper with auto, fixture, and live modes.
Add evidence-safety scanning for raw PII and token material.
Add focused tests for fixture E2E, live fail-fast, and sensitive evidence rejection.
Add scheduled/manual GitHub workflow with deterministic fixture proof and configured live execution.
Add optional launchd template for local recurring monitor operation.
Update runbook, feature registry, service registry, data dictionary, workflow coverage inventory, progress, and backlog mirrors.
Run validation, closeout hook, local CI, PR checks, and issue closeout.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduled/manual monitor path exists for Remote MCP live health.
Authenticated smoke and anonymous rejection checks are represented in the monitor path.
Strict audit HMAC verification and tamper-negative verification are represented in the monitor path.
Evidence handling documents and checks that no secrets, bearer tokens, Cloudflare Access tokens, JWT fragments, or raw PII are preserved.
Runbook documents operator setup, expected outputs, and alert/remediation behavior.
Local tests cover fixture monitor success and fail-fast missing live configuration behavior.
Final AC: E2E testing runs through local CI and PR checks before closeout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Identify required artifacts, ACs, recurring monitor shape, and validation commands for issue #372.
specialist_reviews.summary: The slice should remain an operational follow-up, keep Remote MCP invariants unchanged, add a monitor script/test pair, scheduler surface, runbook/registry artifacts, and prove payload-safe output.

alternate_model_review.summary: Accepted operational monitor scope with no standards rewrite; recommended monitor script/tests, launchd template, runbook/registry updates, validation, and closeout artifacts.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #372 may add a recurring Remote MCP monitor wrapper, tests, workflow, launchd template, documentation, registries, validation evidence, closeout, and generated graph/wiki artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Finish implementation, run validation, open PR, merge after checks pass, close issue #372.

material_deviation_rules: If monitor evidence includes secrets or raw sensitive payloads, remove the evidence, rotate affected credentials if live material was exposed, and rerun validation.
If live monitor cannot run due absent secrets, record fixture and fail-fast evidence only; do not claim live recurring health.
If scheduled workflow fails without production secrets, adjust workflow to skip unconfigured live mode while preserving fixture proof and fail-closed partial configuration.
